concept: Surreal alpine music resort where stage glamour meets snow, soil, and shadow
scene: A mid-century travel-poster vista shows a surreal mountain resort at dusk, where a snowy alpine terrace drops into a theatrical valley under a vast cream-to-teal sky. In the foreground, a black grand piano half-buried in stylised snow sits beside a winding ochre path paved with oversized newspaper-like rectangles, while a slim adult traveler in a checked coat uses a polished walking cane as a conductor’s baton, facing the view in profile. Along the middle ground, dark pines and velvet-curtain-like cliffs frame a flower market pavilion crowned with a glittering tiara-shaped marquee, its bouquets rendered as simplified coral and pink bursts. From a circular garden bed of dark earth rises a monumental afro-shaped topiary mound with a radiant singing face abstracted into sunburst lines, becoming the emotional heart of the landscape without reading as a statue. Overhead, suspended between cable-car towers, a white many-legged wheel turns like a fantastical ski-lift emblem against the big sky. On a shadowed overlook to one side, a few long-haired adult silhouettes gather in deep black coats like mysterious travelers, while a dreamy cluster of close, soft-featured adult faces is reimagined as overlapping portrait banners on the resort walls. Snowy peaks in the distance catch stylised coral light, tying the cabaret glamour, underground surrealism, editorial collage, and winter music landscape into one connected poster world.
prompt: Create one mid-century travel poster illustration - simplified geometry, stylised light, smooth gradient skies, flat confident shapes, and absolutely no lettering. The image is a 16:9 music blog header. Weave in recognisable elements taken from the 8 uploaded album covers: source 1: the side-profile man with the upright cane; source 2: the crowned bouquet-bearing woman; source 3: the snow-covered grand piano in the mountains; source 4: the suspended many-legged circular sculpture; source 5: the shadowy long-haired figures emerging from blackness; source 6: the dreamy overlapping close-up cluster of adult faces; source 7: the afro-framed face rising from dark earth; source 8: the newspaper front-page layout transformed into paving and signage. Transform the subjects, figures, creatures, objects, and symbols depicted in the uploaded covers so they stay recognisable, and place them all inside one cohesive world with a single shared setting and consistent light. Stylise each album motif into the poster world - as landmarks, vehicles, figures, flora, or emblems drawn in the same simplified poster language. Composition: One heroic vista with a strong horizon, a big sky, and clear foreground, middle-ground, and background bands. Colour direction: cream, teal, coral, ochre, midnight black - warm faded lithograph colours - cream, teal, coral, ochre - tuned to the sleeves. Mood: mysterious, theatrical, windswept, and exultant. Keep the scene safe and tasteful: feature only adults (no children, infants, or minors), and show no gore, wounds, body horror, medical or anatomical imagery, blank or milky eyes, nudity, or sexual content. Reinterpret any such source motif abstractly, and suggest rather than exactly replicate the likeness of any identifiable real person. Do not include any text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, or signage anywhere in the image. Avoid: readable text, letters, words, numbers, captions, titles, logos, watermarks, signage, grid layout, contact sheet, evenly tiled squares, raw album-cover thumbnails, collage of separate panels, photorealistic photograph, DSLR photo, 3D render, CGI, octane render, glossy 3D rendering, camera depth of field, lens bokeh, realistic skin texture, a posed group of people arranged for the camera, objects displayed on tables, trays, or plinths, one giant central sculpture as the centrepiece of the scene. Bold, confident, gallery-quality print design with a strong sense of place.
